scenario: Helios Bank runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 13000 transactions per day. After clicking Apply on a batch move spanning 500 process instances, the platform engineer is taken to the instances list view and wants to track how many of those 500 instances have been modified so far without scripting against the engine API.
question: How can the platform engineer track how many of the 500 instances have been modified after the batch starts?

options.text: Open the operations panel on the instances list view and click the Instances link in the batch operation entry to see how many instances have been modified
Open the Console cluster page and read the partition-level event throughput metric, which equals the count of modified process instances for the operation
Open the Tasklist view and filter user tasks by the batch operation id, where each modified instance briefly raises a tracking user task for the operation
Open the Optimize report library and run the Batch Modifications report, which lists the latest modification operations in real time during the cluster operation

a.text: Correct. The batch modification guide says that after the operation is started, the operator can click the Instances link in the operations panel entry to check how many process instances have been modified so far.

b.text: Incorrect. Cluster throughput metrics are unrelated to per-operation batch progress; the documented progress signal is the Instances link in the operations panel for the batch entry.

c.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not raise per-instance tracking user tasks for batch operations; the documented place to check batch progress is the operations panel on the instances list.

d.text: Incorrect. Optimize is an analytics product and is not the documented place for live batch progress; Operate exposes batch progress via the Instances link in the operations panel entry.

explanation: Once the batch modification has started, Operate redirects the operator to the instances list view and exposes batch progress in the operations panel; clicking the Instances link in the batch entry shows how many process instances have been modified.
